title: strychnos rouhamon
intro: Strychnos rouhamon, also known as the Rouham's poison nut or African poison nut, is a plant species in the Loganiaceae family, native to tropical Africa. The medicinal properties of Strychnos rouhamon have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of Strychnos rouhamon include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are typically made by drying and grinding the plant's leaves and seeds.
